title: DTN methods: time module imported inside functions
description:
File: python/src/lichen/routing/router.py:656, 717

```python
def dtn_buffer_message(...):
    import time
    now_unix = int(time.time())
    ...

def dtn_expire_old(...):
    import time
    now_unix = int(time.time())
```

WHY IT'S A BUG (minor):
1. Repeated import overhead on every call
2. Makes the dependency non-obvious at module level
3. Harder to mock for testing (must patch inside the method)
4. Inconsistent with the rest of the codebase which imports at top

IMPACT: Minor performance overhead; testing difficulty; code style inconsistency.

FIX: Move import to top of file and use consistently:
```python
import time  # at top of file
...
def dtn_buffer_message(...):
    now_unix = int(time.time())
```

Or better, accept now_unix as a parameter for testability:
```python
def dtn_expire_old(self, now_unix: int | None = None) -> int:
    if now_unix is None:
        import time
        now_unix = int(time.time())
```
close_reason: Fixed: time module is already imported at module level (line 18) and used correctly in dtn_buffer_message (line 661) and dtn_expire_old (line 721). No inline imports remain.
